Given constraints, we can make a tool_call to session.expand_file with target selector for the file and maybe depth "symbols" to get details. Then we can capture acquired_fact_ids referencing short ids that we will later use.

We need to output JSON now with a tool_call. So we need to pick a tool name exactly as listed. Let's use session.expand_file@v1.

Payload: target selector maybe "crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs". Also maybe set depth "2" and relation_filters maybe ["exports"]? But we can keep simple.

We also need to provide acquired_fact_ids: an array of fact ids we are about to acquire. Since we haven't acquired yet, maybe we can pre-emptively list something? The instruction says acquired_fact_ids: ["<fact id>"]. Should we include placeholder? Probably we should list the ids we plan to acquire in this step. We can include something like "F1" or "S5"? But those are graph frontier nodes; they are short ids. Use "F1" maybe refers to the file node? Actually in graph frontier, they refer to focus node as crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs (maybe represented as some short id?). Not sure.

Better to acquire after the tool call; maybe we can include empty array for now? But we need to fill with something. Could leave empty array.

Readiness_state: we are not ready to edit yet, we need evidence. So readiness_state could be "evidence_visible" or "evidence_acquired"? Since we haven't yet acquired evidence, maybe "evidence_visible" if we have some visible evidence already. But we might set "not_ready". The contract says readiness_state can be "not_ready|evidence_visible|evidence_acquired|ready_to_edit". We are preparing to gather evidence, so maybe "evidence_visible" if we already have some visible evidence (like we have expanded file earlier). But we still need exact schema version definitions.

I'll choose "evidence_visible" indicating we have some evidence but not enough.

Thus JSON:

{
  "kind": "tool_call",
  "assistant_message": "Expanding artifacts.rs to capture schema version definitions",
  "tool_call": {
    "tool_name": "session.expand_file@v1",
    "payload": {
      "target": "crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs",
      "depth": "2",
      "relation_filters": ["exports"]
    }
  },
  "acquired_fact_ids": [],
  "readiness_state": "evidence_visible"
}

But need to ensure correct JSON shape: {{"kind":"think|tool_call|complete","assistant_message":"<brief status>","tool_call":{...},"acquired_fact_ids":["<fact id>"],"readiness_state":"..."}}

Note they used double braces in description but actual output should be JSON only, not double wrapped. So output a single JSON object.

Thus:

{
  "kind": "tool_call",
  "assistant_message": "Expanding artifacts.rs to capture schema version definitions",
  "tool_call": {
    "tool_name": "session.expand_file@v1",
    "payload": {
      "target": "crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs",
      "depth": "2",
      "relation_filters": ["exports"]
    }
  },
  "acquired_fact_ids": [],
  "readiness_state": "evidence_visible"
}

Make sure no extra fields. Ensure proper quoting. No trailing commas.

Let's produce that.
